description: Tier 1b fresh-decls task set for the Bridge Experiment (docs/research/BRIDGE-EXPERIMENT.md). Theorems added to Mathlib master AFTER the Brain decl snapshot, with every retrieval index pinned older => held out from all arms (including the Wikibrain). TheoremGraph recipe at larger n.

held_out.guarantee: every kept decl name is ABSENT from both the pinned decl universe (statement_formal.csv, 388105 decls) and the Brain nodes (brain/data/nodes.jsonl), and was added to Mathlib master strictly after the pin date.

grading_note: gold_formal ends `:= sorry` and is the exact Mathlib master statement. It uses standard Mathlib section-variable/open style, so it typechecks standalone only after prepending gold_context (the enclosing variable/open/universe lines, given in scope order) under gold_header; a superset header `import Mathlib` also works. Grading = BEq+/equivalence vs gold (typecheck is never success), per the design.
determinacy_note: Determinacy is a single-annotator first-pass screen (the design calls for two independent formalizers). The determinate fraction is high because candidates were pre-filtered to docstringed, self-contained statements and bespoke-helper/unrecoverable-hypothesis decls were dropped outright rather than flagged. Flagged rows are genuine near-duplicates or informally-underdetermined variants; they are excluded from primary analysis.
